start: ^The room is cramped, smelling of stale coffee and old paper. Through the half-open door, the polished mahogany of the front desk is visible.
^Eric's jacket is still draped over the back of a chair, as if he'll be back any second. But we both know he won't.
^Morigan is leaning against a stack of filing cabinets, her arms crossed. She looks completely out of place in this dingy backroom.

s: ^But that's exactly why I want to leave.
^He looked like he’d been wandering for weeks, not an hour...

c-0: ^Morigan sighs, clearly annoyed that I'm not paying attention.
^I am *not* accepting your resignation. Not after Eric decided this is a good time to have an 'accident'.
c-1: ^I try to argue that I don't want to stay and go through whatever Eric has experienced down there.
^Oh, please. What's the big drama? He went missing for an hour and turned up in the basement.
^The man probably tripped and got turned around in the dark. It’s a maze down there, nothing more.
c-2: ^I describe how gaunt and dehydrated he looked, like the life had been sucked right out of him.
^It’s the boiler room, darling! It’s a furnace in there. Of course he’d look a bit... weathered. A glass of water and a nap, and he'll be fine.
^She says it with a sharp confidence, but I catch the way her gaze wavers momentarily.
^Look, I get it. You're a bit rattled.
^I can't let you quit... not when I'm this close to selling off this money-pit. I won't let a 'staffing crisis' tank the inspection.
^There's a mystery guest lurking around, and everything depends on them not noticing a single whiff of chaos. If they report 'instability', the buyers will walk.
c-3: ^Yes. Who'd have known that running a hotel would be this boring and, most importantly, costly!?
^The 'Mistress of the Grand Morgana' had a nice ring to it, but the novelty has worn thin. I've had enough of 'upholding the legacy'.
^Maybe I'll open a high-end boutique in the capital after this. Something that doesn't involve managing a literal maze of rooms.
^Three nights. That's all I need from you.
c-4: ^I dunno! Just keep the lights on and the smiles fake until the inspection is over.
^After that, you can stay, quit, go wherever you want. But until then, you aren't going anywhere.
^Without waiting for a response, she pivots on her heels, her heels clicking sharply against the linoleum floor as she exits the staff room.
^A moment later, the roar of an engine fades into the winter night.
^Silence returns to the staff room, heavier than before.
^My eyes fall on the desk. Amidst the clutter of receipts and half-empty mugs, there's a leather-bound folder I've never seen before.
^Eric always kept it locked in the top drawer. Now, the drawer is hanging open.
c-5: ^The moment my fingers touch the worn leather, the old vacuum-tube TV in the corner hums to life.
^Static flickers across the screen, then settles into a high-contrast, grainy cartoon.
^A cheerful, bobble-headed bellhop character appears on the screen, waving frantically. The music is a tinny, upbeat orchestral track from the 40s.
^Greetings, New Recruit! Welcome to the Grand Morgana family!
^Being a Night Manager is a snap! Just follow these simple rules to ensure a 'Splendid Stay' for our guests!
^RULE 1: Keep it Tidy! A clean hotel is a happy hotel. Don't let the grime settle!
^RULE 2: Stay Vigilant! Patrol the hotel, and keep an eye out for anything that doesn't feel right.

s: ^RULE 3: FIX IT.** If something looks odd, out of place, or just plain 'wrong'... put it back where it belongs! No questions asked!

c-0: ^RULE 4: Stay Connected! If the desk phone rings, it means somebody's got a request to make!

c-6: ^Oh, right. Kids these days have a... what's that? A smartie phone. Eh, it's all the same. Check your little 'app' on your 'phone', then.
^Now, for the finishing touch! A manager isn't a manager without his authority!
^Reach out and take it! Put on the badge of the Grand Hotel Morgana Night Manager!
^The TV screen stays on, showing the Bellhop pointing directly at the desk in front of me.
^A silver badge, tarnished but still gleaming, has appeared where the manual was.
c-7: ^[Open your INVENTORY to view the badge. Select 'EQUIP' to officially begin your shift.]
^Splendid! You look just like the real thing! Welcome aboard, Manager! We're *so* glad you're staying with us.
^The TV screen cuts to black with a sharp 'pop'.
^The room is quiet again, but the silver badge feels heavy on my chest.
^The exit is right there. Time to see what's actually waiting in the lobby.
